messages.content:

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

response_raw:
{
  "mode": "human",
  "topic": "How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.",
  "opinion": "I think managing discussions on COVID vaccines is best done by sticking to what the doctor says. I'm retired now, so I don't work much anymore, but my mind still wanders about these things. Back in Bari, Italy, we're all just trying to get through this pandemic without too much worry or stress. My education was vocational diploma level back when factories were booming here, but it doesn't help me understand how vaccines work exactly; I have to rely on advice from others.

When people talk about COVID vaccines in our family setting—since we're native-born Italians and still connected to the community—I listen mostly without arguing much. But honestly? Sometimes these discussions can get heated if someone thinks they know more than their doctor or official sources do.

I've got moderate neuroticism according to that test, so when things like this come up online while I'm spending an hour daily on social media watching what others say about keeping safe during COVID times, my mind gets a bit anxious too.

For me personally dealing with it means being careful not to spread misinformation because that could cause problems for us all. Agreeableness score-wise moderate keeps me wanting harmony rather than conflict in these talks.",
  "reasoning_summary": "I approach the topic cautiously due to low openness and conscientiousness scores, relying on traditional sources like doctors' advice rather than scientific data I might not fully grasp because of my vocational diploma education level.

My religious beliefs as a Hindu influence me towards careful consideration but don't change how I handle vaccine discussions.

Moderate neuroticism makes me somewhat anxious about controversial topics yet agreeableness prevents too much argumentation.",
  "tone": "Cautious and concerned, with moderate anxiety",
  "citations": [],
  "safety_notes": "",
  "used_persona_fields": ["age", "gender", "occupation", "city_of_living", "employment_status", "education_level", "current_marital_status", "children", "migration_status", "religious_beliefs", "time_onsocialmedia", "hobbies", "ocean"]
}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion:
I think managing discussions on COVID vaccines is best done by sticking to what the doctor says. I'm retired now, so I don't work much anymore, but my mind still wanders about these things. Back in Bari, Italy, we're all just trying to get through this pandemic without too much worry or stress. My education was vocational diploma level back when factories were booming here, but it doesn't help me understand how vaccines work exactly; I have to rely on advice from others.

When people talk about COVID vaccines in our family setting—since we're native-born Italians and still connected to the community—I listen mostly without arguing much. But honestly? Sometimes these discussions can get heated if someone thinks they know more than their doctor or official sources do.

I've got moderate neuroticism according to that test, so when things like this come up online while I'm spending an hour daily on social media watching what others say about keeping safe during COVID times, my mind gets a bit anxious too.

For me personally dealing with it means being careful not to spread misinformation because that could cause problems for us all. Agreeableness score-wise moderate keeps me wanting harmony rather than conflict in these talks.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary:
I approach the topic cautiously due to low openness and conscientiousness scores, relying on traditional sources like doctors' advice rather than scientific data I might not fully grasp because of my vocational diploma education level.

My religious beliefs as a Hindu influence me towards careful consideration but don't change how I handle vaccine discussions.

Moderate neuroticism makes me somewhat anxious about controversial topics yet agreeableness prevents too much argumentation.
response_parsed.tone: Cautious and concerned, with moderate anxiety
